preamble: The close back rounded vowel /u/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to the close front unrounded vowel /i/ through a process involving both fronting and unrounding. This shift is attested in certain historical contexts, often conditioned by specific phonetic environments.

examples.note: Shift of short /u/ to /i/ in closed syllables.

sources: Kutscher, E. Y. (1974). The Language and Linguistic Background of the Isaiah Scroll (IQlIsaa). Studies on the Texts of the Desert of Judah, vol. 6. Leiden: Brill.
